name: text-embedding-3-large
summary: text-embedding-3-large is OpenAI's highest-quality text embedding model, producing 3072-dimensional dense vectors that outperform the previous ada-002 model on the MTEB benchmark. It supports native dimensionality truncation, allowing users to trade off between vector quality and storage cost.
use_when: Production retrieval-augmented generation pipelines where MTEB leaderboard performance directly impacts search quality. Applications requiring a single API endpoint for multiple languages without managing separate embedding models. Systems where dimensionality truncation can reduce vector database costs while maintaining competitive accuracy.
avoid_when: On-premise or air-gapped environments where API dependency is unacceptable. High-volume batch processing where per-token API costs exceed the cost of self-hosting an open-source model. Use cases requiring full control over the embedding model for domain-specific fine-tuning.
pros: Top-tier MTEB performance with strong retrieval accuracy across classification, clustering, and semantic search benchmarks
Native support for dimensionality truncation down to 256 dimensions, enabling flexible storage and latency trade-offs without retraining
Multilingual capability with competitive performance in 100+ languages through a single API endpoint, simplifying architecture
Consistent API behavior and uptime guarantees from OpenAI's infrastructure, reducing operational burden for engineering teams
cons: API-only access with no local weights available, creating vendor lock-in and preventing offline or air-gapped deployment
Per-token pricing accumulates quickly for high-volume batch embedding jobs, often exceeding the cost of self-hosted alternatives
Cannot be fine-tuned on domain-specific corpora, limiting adaptability to specialized vocabularies like legal, medical, or scientific text
Rate limits and quota restrictions can throttle large-scale ingestion pipelines, requiring complex retry and batching logic

key_hyperparams.name: dimensions
key_hyperparams.note: Output embedding dimension. Can be truncated to 256, 512, 1024, or 2048. Lower dimensions reduce storage but may hurt retrieval accuracy on hard negatives.

key_hyperparams.name: encoding_format
key_hyperparams.note: Output format: 'float' for standard 32-bit floats, 'base64' for compressed string encoding. Use 'base64' when transmitting embeddings over JSON to reduce payload size.

quick_start:
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI()
response = client.embeddings.create(
    model='text-embedding-3-large',
    input='The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog',
    dimensions=1024,
    encoding_format='float'
)
embedding = response.data[0].embedding
print(len(embedding))  # 1024
